title: Choroidal Neovascular Membrane (Type 3)
description: Type 3 neovascularization, or retinal angiomatous proliferation, arises from the deep retina and extends toward the RPE and choroid. On OCT it is characterised by intraretinal hyper-reflective foci, cystic edema, pigment epithelial detachment, and associated subretinal or intraretinal fluid.
